author: OSUN
msg:
I’m not exactly tech savvy so forgive me if this is a stupid question. 
Is there a way to have more than one IP address on a single router?  I called Verizon yesterday and got a newer router (needed an upgrade anyway) but the guy didn’t seem to understand what I was asking and I didn’t feel like explaining the reason. 
Should I just sign up for internet with another provider?  
This is all just so I can run another 20 phones by the way.

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: Only if you have a proxy forwarding program on your router, or if your router takes a SIM card. Otherwise you'll only have the one IP your internet provider gives you
